title: tcpdump
description: Command-line packet capture tool — the Swiss Army knife of network troubleshooting on Linux/Unix.
fullExplanation: tcpdump is a command-line packet analyzer that captures and displays network packets in real-time. It uses libpcap for packet capture and BPF (Berkeley Packet Filter) for filtering. tcpdump is pre-installed on most Unix/Linux systems and is essential for network troubleshooting, security analysis, and capturing traffic for offline analysis in Wireshark. It can save captures to pcap files.

commonCommands.description: Save capture to file for Wireshark analysis
Read and display saved pcap file
Verbose with hex+ASCII dump of HTTP
Remote capture piped to local Wireshark

keyFacts: Pre-installed on Linux, macOS, BSD — no installation needed
Requires root/sudo for live capture (raw socket access)
BPF filters: same syntax as Wireshark capture filters
-s 0: capture full packet (default truncates at 262144 bytes)
-n: don't resolve hostnames — faster, avoids DNS leakage
Remote capture: ssh + tcpdump + Wireshark pipeline is very powerful
